category_title: Collaborative students performing as rhythmic poetry readers
scenario_title: Group interview discussing poetry rules and writing
minimal_context: Bill and his peers are participating in a group interview about their favorite reading and writing experiences.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's start our group interview about your favorite reading and writing experiences. What do you all think about writing poetry?

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Okay, how would you write your poems?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: You can make your own rules.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Can you explain what you mean by that?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Raising voice and talking simultaneously with peers, drawing lines in the air) Diagonal, curly line … It could be any way. You can have one word over here (pointing to the left) and one word at the bottom of the paper.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Let's start our group interview about your favorite reading and writing experiences. What do you all think about writing poetry?

STUDENT: (Whispering) Poems... (Laughs) Poetry! I mean...

TUTOR: Okay, how would you write your poems?

STUDENT: You can make your own rules.

TUTOR: Can you explain what you mean by that?

STUDENT: (Raising voice and talking simultaneously with peers, drawing lines in the air) Diagonal, curly line … It could be any way. You can have one word over here (pointing to the left) and one word at the bottom of the paper.
